spec_match.rationale: The menu bar uses focusgroup="menubar" with nested focusgroup="menu" submenus, and the comparison panel now contains an actual focusgroup menubar.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified Tab entry, Left/Right top-level movement, Enter/Space submenu opening, Up/Down submenu movement, Escape return, and Tab exit.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page copy and comparison labels identify menubar as the behavior token and demonstrate the Tab-flood problem without focusgroup.

edge_cases.rationale: MCP verified all four submenus, Escape close/return behavior, Tab exit, and clean console/network state.

references.rationale: The page retains the required ChromeStatus reference link.

design_system.rationale: The flat bordered menu, log, and comparison panels continue to use shared CSS variables and explicit button text colors.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP verification used Chrome Canary 151 with experimental web platform features enabled. Top-level menubar and nested submenu keyboard behavior passed for File, Edit, View, and Help, and console/network checks were clean.
